data.body: EXPERIMENT: Sonnet-scored sim on 48 labeled plan-review findings (38 VALID / 10 BORDERLINE, both high-severity). RESULT: VALID vs BORDERLINE is NOT impact-separable (every model incl. old ~AUC 0.50; best single sub-q S3-decomposition 0.60; some axes anti-correlate). => the residual VALID/BORDERLINE boundary is altitude/already-handled/urgency, NOT impact magnitude, so an impact-model rework adds little SEPARATION there. Sub-question reliability was ~1.0 (decomposition works). The one evidence-consistent, research-backed change that directly fixes the ORIGINAL band-stranding complaint: replace the diluting MEAN with SEVERITY-FIRST MAX + hard override (FMEA Action-Priority) so a single high axis isn't averaged below the bar — no new axes needed. Optionally add the detection amplifier (silent-vs-self-revealing was reliable, AUC-neutral here) and wire the now-deterministic decomposition signal (G5 fix). NOTE: plan-impact rework is LOWER priority than the already-decided 0.60 threshold-lowering + G5 determinism, which capture the stranded findings directly.
